div1.entryFree: Sore, adv. grievously, violently: one s. sick, Ven. 702. John V, 4, 6. H4B IV, 3, 83. her senses s. dismayed, Ven. 896 (the earlier Qq all dismayed). I'll fear no other thing so s. Merch. V, 307. your sorrow was too s. laid on, Wint. V, 3, 49. we see the wind sit s. upon our sails, R2 II, 1, 265. his soul shall stand s. charged, H5 I, 2, 283. s. hurt and bruised, Troil. V, 5, 14. too s. enpierced with his shaft, Rom. I, 4, 19. O bill, s. shaming those rich-left heirs, Cymb. IV, 2, 225.
